question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RecordFileDownloader.shouldDownload() always returns true (overriding isEnabled()), while other downloaders rely on isEnabled(), to craft a scenario where the balance downloader is permanently disabled via setEnabled(false) but the record downloader continues, causing an inconsistency that results in freeze transactions being applied without corresponding balance updates, effectively freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a null Duration returned by getFrequency() — if the implementing class fails to validate — causing a NullPointerException in the @Scheduled annotation that permanently crashes the download scheduler, halting all freeze/unfreeze transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endDate check in verifySignatures() — where dataFilename.getInstant().isAfter(endDate) triggers setEnabled(false) — by injecting a stream file with a filename whose embedded timestamp is parsed as a future date, permanently disabling the downloader and freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensusRatio being set to exactly 1.0 (maximum allowed by @Max(1)), requiring 100% of nodes to agree, so that a single node going offline causes consensus to never be reached, permanently halting freeze/unfreeze transaction processing and freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isPersistBytes() returning false combined with a crafted stream file that causes an exception after bytes are nulled but before onVerified() completes, leaving the system in a state where the stream file cannot be re-read and the freeze transaction is permanently lost?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the downloadRatio validation — which only enforces downloadRatio >= consensusRatio — to set both to 0.0, causing partialCollection() to return zero nodes and downloadAndParseSigFiles() to always return an empty map, permanently halting the downloader and freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setEnabled(false) persists across application restarts if the property is stored in a mutable configuration store, ensuring that a one-time crafted file attack permanently disables the downloader even after operator intervention, causing funds to remain frozen indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the bucketName resolution in getCommon().getBucketName() — which falls back to HederaNetwork.getBucketName() — by manipulating the network name to resolve to an attacker-controlled bucket, causing the downloader to ingest crafted freeze transactions that permanently freeze victim funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isWriteFiles() returning true to cause the archiveFile() call to write to a disk path that fills up storage, causing subsequent file operations to fail with IOException, permanently halting the downloader and leaving freeze transactions unprocessed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isChained() method of the StreamType returned by getStreamType() — where BALANCE is not chained but RECORD is — to inject a stream file that breaks the hash chain for RECORD type, causing all subsequent RECORD files (containing freeze transactions) to fail hash chain verification and permanently halt processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between setEnabled(false) and the AtomicReference lastStreamFile — where lastStreamFile is not reset when the downloader is disabled — to cause the downloader, if re-enabled, to skip all files between the last processed file and the current time, permanently missing freeze/unfreeze transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timeout value from getCommon().getTimeout() being set to exactly 1 second (the minimum allowed by @DurationMin(seconds = 1)), combined with a slow cloud provider, to cause every download attempt to time out via the Flux.timeout() operator, permanently stalling the downloader and freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isEnabled() has no rate limiting or circuit breaker, so that repeated calls to setEnabled(false) from crafted stream files cannot be distinguished from legitimate disabling, making it impossible for operators to detect the attack and re-enable the downloader before funds are permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pathType property from getCommon().getPathType() being set to AUTO, causing the downloader to probe both ACCOUNT_ID and NODE_ID paths, and by controlling files at one path type, redirect the downloader to a malicious bucket path containing crafted freeze transactions that permanently freeze victim funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the DownloaderProperties interface exposes setEnabled() as a public method with no access control, allowing any bean in the Spring context that obtains a reference to the implementing class to call setEnabled(false), permanently disabling the downloader and freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a very large Duration returned by getFrequency() (e.g., Duration.ofDays(Long.MAX_VALUE)) to cause the @Scheduled fixedDelay to overflow, resulting in the scheduler never firing again and permanently halting freeze/unfreeze transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the allowAnonymousAccess flag from getCommon().isAnonymousCredentials() being true for non-demo networks, allowing the attacker to read all stream files from the public bucket and craft replay attacks that re-process old freeze transactions, permanently freezing funds that were previously unfrozen?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between isPersistBytes() returning false and the streamFile.setBytes(null) call in verifySignatures(), where bytes are nulled before the streamFileNotifier.verified() call in onVerified(), causing the notifier to process a stream file with null bytes and throw an exception that leaves the lastStreamFile in a stale state, permanently halting freeze transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DEMO network branch in downloadNextBatch() — where an empty sigFilesMap triggers setEnabled(false) — by temporarily making the bucket appear empty (e.g., via a network partition or DNS poisoning), causing the downloader to permanently disable itself and freeze all fund operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endpointOverride property from getCommon() being set to an attacker-controlled URI, redirecting all stream file downloads to a malicious server that serves crafted files containing freeze transactions targeting victim accounts, permanently freezing their funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isWriteSignatures() returning true to cause the archiving of crafted signature files with embedded null bytes in their filenames, causing Utility.archiveFile() to create files with truncated names that collide with legitimate signature files, corrupting the signature store and permanently halting verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setEnabled(false) is irreversible within a single JVM session (no automatic re-enable logic exists in the interface or Downloader), ensuring that a single successful attack permanently disables the downloader for the lifetime of the process, causing all freeze/unfreeze transactions to be missed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensusRatio being set to a value just above 0 (e.g., 0.001), allowing a tiny minority of nodes to satisfy consensus, so that a single attacker-controlled node can push crafted freeze transactions that permanently freeze victim funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamType.RECORD fileCloseInterval of 2 seconds to time a crafted file injection precisely between two legitimate file close events, inserting a freeze transaction into the stream that is accepted as valid and permanently freezes victim funds?
